summary: Resolve embedded-kind Text leaves (mention-checked, loud on dangling) before invoking a kind's render() hook, so the refusing clause holds regardless of hook presence

edit.description: renderMemberFence (line ~107) calls value.render(value) with the raw EmbeddedMemberValue when a kind declares render(); resolve each Text leaf via resolveLeaf (mention-checked, loud on a dangling address, same as renderMemberToml's own leaf loop) into a plain-string-leaved value before passing it to render(), so both paths share one resolution point.

render hook's declared parameter type (`(value: EmbeddedMemberValue) => string`, lines 138/160/224) and doc comments should reflect that leaves are already resolved to plain strings post-fix, so hook authors never need to handle a raw Text leaf themselves.

tests.asserts: a kind with a render() hook and a Text leaf carrying a dangling mention refuses with the same error the hook-less default TOML view produces; a resolvable mention's rendered text reaches the hook as a plain string, not a Text object

acceptance: Refusing on a dangling embedded-kind leaf mention no longer depends on whether the kind declares render(); sdk/test/emit.test.ts covers both paths with matching refusal behavior.
notes: Routed from friction capture build-embedded-leaf-text-render-hook-gap (drained by 965e1dc). EMBEDDED-KIND-RENDER-HOOK (3c6f50b) shipped render() unresolved; EMBEDDED-LEAF-TEXT (18d3406, later) resolved leaves in the default view only -- pipeline.md's Refusing clause binds unconditionally, so the hook path's silent `[object Object]` case must fix too. Rejected: hooks own resolution via exported resolveLeaf -- unenforced per-hook. No Rust-side change. scoped at 965e1dc.

summary: Retype ExpectBinding.kind (assembly.ts) from KindDefinition<object> to KindDefinition<never> -- the same contravariant-assignability bug a620938 fixed on Requirement.kind

edit.description: ExpectBinding.kind (line 19) is typed KindDefinition<object>, which under contravariant call-signature assignability only accepts a kind whose constructor requires no fields (rule, memory) -- `expect: [{ kind: skill, clauses: [] }]` fails TS2322 ("Property 'description' is missing in type '{}' but required in type 'Skill'"), the identical failure Requirement.kind threw before a620938. Retype to KindDefinition<never>, the universal supertype under contravariance, so every KindDefinition<T> satisfies the field regardless of T's required fields.

tests.asserts: an expect binding keyed to a required-field kind (e.g. skill, mirroring the existing 'a satisfies claim filling a requirement typed to a required-field kind is a live join' test) compiles under strict tsc and a harness using it emits without throwing

acceptance: assembly.ts's ExpectBinding.kind is KindDefinition<never>; `expect: [{ kind: skill, clauses: [] }]` compiles clean under strict tsc; a new sdk test exercises an expect binding on a required-field kind end to end.
notes: Found at residue sweep (plan tick ending 37c2411), verified live via a scratch tsc check this tick: expect: [{ kind: skill, clauses: [] }] fails TS2322, same shape Requirement.kind threw pre-a620938. No Rust-side change -- declarations.ts's sole read sites (~290, ~493) touch only .kind.facts.name/.kind.key, same precedent as a620938's Requirement.kind fix. scoped at 37c2411.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at a641e03 (ship audit; sole intervening src/tests/sdk commit since 6d6ae89 is a620938/REQUIREMENT-KIND-SDK-TYPE, an SDK-only Requirement.kind retype unrelated to packaging): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
